problem_addressed: Evaluating machine learning agents on machine learning engineering tasks through a structured benchmark framework.
core_claim: MLE-bench provides a comprehensive framework for evaluating ML agents on ML engineering tasks, demonstrated through a scalar coupling prediction competition.
key_contributions: Introduction of MLE-bench as an evaluation framework for ML agents
Log-MAE evaluation metric for scalar coupling prediction tasks
Structured submission and evaluation procedures for ML engineering benchmarks
novelty: MLE-bench as a novel framework specifically designed to evaluate ML agents on ML engineering tasks, with the scalar coupling prediction competition serving as the benchmark implementation.
method_summary: The paper presents MLE-bench which evaluates ML agents on predicting scalar coupling constants between atom pairs in molecules using the Log of Mean Absolute Error (Log-MAE) metric, calculated per coupling type and averaged across types.
method_components: Log-MAE evaluation metric with mathematical formulation
Molecule-based train/test split ensuring no molecule appears in both sets
Multiple data sources including XYZ structure files, dipole moments, magnetic shielding tensors, Mulliken charges, and scalar coupling contributions
key_innovation: The Log-MAE metric that provides equal weight to improvements across different scalar coupling types, with a floor of '1e-9' for MAE and theoretical minimum score of approximately -20.7232 for perfect predictions.
assumptions: The molecule-based train/test split ensures proper generalization evaluation
The provided data sources are sufficient for accurate scalar coupling prediction
The Log-MAE metric appropriately captures model performance across different coupling types
theoretical_grounding: The paper provides the mathematical formulation of the Log-MAE metric: score = (1/T) * Σ[log(1/n_t * Σ|y_i - ŷ_i|)], where T is the number of coupling types, n_t is the number of observations of type t, y_i is the actual constant, and ŷ_i is the predicted constant.
metrics_used: Log of Mean Absolute Error (Log MAE)
headline_result: o1-preview achieved 34.3% medals in low complexity competitions
best_result: o1-preview achieved 34.3% medals in low complexity competitions
title: MLE-bench: Evaluating Machine Learning Agents on Machine Learning Engineering
